rules.description: Produce natural-sounding translations that capture intended meaning for a wide Japanese-speaking audience
rules.notes: Avoid literal translation. Balance faithfulness to source and readability in target language

rules.description: Use clear, concise and precise style that is easily understood and doesn't read like a translation
rules.notes: Put yourself in the reader's place to ensure clarity and fluency

rules.description: Avoid heaviness, ambiguities, repetitions, and overly polite expressions
rules.notes: Also avoid enriching the text excessively

rules.description: Translation of buttons should be clear and concise with attention to character/space limitations
rules.example_incorrect: ミーティングに参加する
rules.notes: English: Join a Meeting

rules.description: Use concise button text avoiding unnecessary particles
rules.notes: English: Join Now

rules.section: Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Check reference material and terminology lists before translating product/application names as they may be trademarked
rules.notes: Terms marked as DNT (Do not translate) should not be localized

rules.description: Replace English examples with Japanese equivalents when needed for localization/adaptation
rules.notes: Example: wildcard operators should use Japanese terms instead of English ones

rules.description: Be precise, clear, consistent and natural. Rephrase unclear sentences and rework sentences that sound like translations
rules.notes: Break up long English sentences into smaller parts for better readability

rules.description: Use accurate and concise style with no ambiguities or mistranslations
rules.notes: Most readers don't have patience for long sentences. Information should be grasped in seconds

rules.description: Write clearly and coherently so target readers understand easily
Be objective and neutral without inserting personal opinions
rules.notes: Avoid colloquial or slang words, be politically correct

rules.description: Ensure content and numbers are free of mistakes
Use idiomatically correct verbs, metaphors, and expressions so text doesn't sound like a translation
Be complete - do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs)
Use です/ます調 in general, except for legal and other documents that need だ/である調
rules.notes: Japanese specific instruction

rules.description: In lists, use だ/である調 or 体言止め
rules.notes: Japanese specific instruction

rules.description: Style should be formal and polite appropriate for business communication
rules.example_correct: ユーザー名を入力してください。
rules.example_incorrect: ユーザー名をご入力ください。
rules.notes: Avoid casual expressions and overly polite expressions like ございます、していただきます

rules.description: Cross-references shall be referenced consistently throughout translation and all files
rules.notes: Translation of document titles might be included in terminology list or reference material

rules.description: If unable to retrieve cross-reference information, contact Project Management team or Language Lead
rules.notes: If no response in time, leave title in source language with proposed translation in parenthesis

rules.description: If document has not been translated before, leave as in source language
Possessives like 'your' and 'our' do not need to be translated unless needed for clarification
rules.example_correct: ファイルを編集します
rules.example_incorrect: あなたのファイルを編集します
rules.notes: English: You can edit your files

rules.description: Use appropriate Japanese expressions instead of direct translation of possessives
rules.example_correct: お使いのコンピューター
rules.example_incorrect: あなたのコンピューター
rules.notes: English: Your computer

rules.description: Usually avoid 'we' or 'us' and replace with client names instead, per Japanese convention
rules.notes: Exception: For RingCentral partner content, this rule doesn't apply. Don't replace 'we'/'us' with RingCentral

rules.description: Subject word doesn't always need translation in Japanese, especially 'you'
rules.notes: Omit 'you' or replace with specific noun. Substitute 'we' with specific noun like company name

rules.description: When subject is a computer or application, omit the subject or use passive voice
rules.example_incorrect: システムが「入力」と表示したら、
rules.notes: English: When the system displays Enter, ...

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables
rules.notes: Updated on 20241207

rules.description: In principle, leave no space between single byte and double byte characters
rules.notes: English: HD video conferencing

rules.description: Exception for URLs: Add a single byte space before and after a URL
Compound Katakana words: No spaces between Katakana words unless necessary to clarify meaning
rules.notes: English: audio device

rules.description: There should be a space between two English words/numbers as appropriate in English grammar
Use straight apostrophe consistently instead of curly apostrophe

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte Japanese period 。 for all Japanese sentences

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte English period . only in English quotation, codes, etc.

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte Japanese comma 、 for all Japanese sentences

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte English comma , only as thousand separator in numbers

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte question mark ？

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte exclamation mark ！
rules.notes: When multiple exclamation marks are used consecutively, use single byte characters

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte tilde ～ for range when minus sign is typically used in English

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte colon : but not after full sentences

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte semi-colon ; only in code and English text, not in Japanese text

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double byte & between two double byte characters, and single byte between two single byte characters

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte double quotation marks "" within Japanese brackets and in quotation when needed
rules.notes: Do not use as general quotation

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Do not use single quotation marks '' in Japanese text

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte Japanese brackets 「」 for quotation, titles, and emphasis where double quotation marks are used in English

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use double-byte Japanese double brackets 『』 for publication titles and cases where differentiation with 「」 is needed

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte brackets [] for UI buttons, tabs, etc.

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte parenthesis ()

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte % with no space between numbers and the symbol
rules.notes: If spelled out as 'percent' in source, using パーセント is OK

rules.section: Punctuation, Brackets, Parentheses, and Other Symbols
rules.description: Use single byte en dash – and em dash — following English source
Japanese abbreviation should be avoided unless widely used and accepted as a Japanese word
rules.notes: スマホ is considered casual and should be avoided except in ad-copy

rules.description: Acronyms should not be translated in general, unless not used in Japan at all
When acronym is before its expansion, include Japanese expansion in parentheses
rules.notes: English: CSV (Comma Separated Values)

rules.description: When expansion comes before acronym, translate expansion and keep English acronym
rules.example_correct: ローカルエリアネットワーク (LAN)
rules.notes: English: local area network (LAN)

rules.description: When acronym is not followed by expansion, use the acronym without expansion
rules.notes: English: OLE

rules.description: Use single byte numbers
Use single byte comma as thousand separator
Kansuji (一, 二, etc.) should not be used unless it's a fixed expression that cannot be converted to regular number
For units not commonly used in Japan, add converted metric value first and leave original in brackets, or convert to metric equivalent
rules.notes: Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches as exception

rules.description: Units should be abbreviated when used with numbers, but can be spelled out if needed
rules.notes: Some units are not commonly abbreviated in Japanese and should always be spelled out

rules.description: Always use Western calendar dates, not Japanese 元号
Use long format 2017年1月12日 in general sentences
Use short format 2017/1/12 in tables or when space is limited
Use long format 午後1時30分 for time
Use short format 13:30 (24h format preferred) when space is limited
Use long format 米ドル in sentences
rules.notes: USD → 米ドル

rules.description: Use short format USD in tables or when space is limited
When source uses currency symbols, use symbols in translation
Do not use italics in Japanese. Use bold or underline for emphasis instead
URLs should be localized if applicable
Translate country and city names based on Japanese conventions
Localize address formats based on Japanese conventions
RingCentral phone numbers should be localized when appropriate using local office numbers
Use xxx-xxx-xxxx format for phone numbers
For overseas numbers, use +Country code-xxx-xxx-xxxx (name of country) format
Write compound Katakana words without spaces or Nakaguro between them
rules.example_correct: モバイルネットワーク

rules.description: Exception: If official company/organization name has Nakaguro or space, use the official name as is
If company has official name in Japan, use the official name. If not represented in Japan, use source text as is
rules.notes: Do not transcribe the name

rules.description: Leave person names in source language unless well-known (politician, celebrity)
rules.notes: Avoid inaccurate transcription

rules.description: If katakana string becomes too long and hard to read, rephrase adding particles like の, による, etc.
Follow national guidelines and add 長音棒 after words ending with -er, -or, -ry, etc.
rules.example_correct: コンピューター, モニター, ユーザー, デリバリー
rules.example_incorrect: コンピュータ, モニタ, ユーザ, デリバリ
rules.notes: Use Microsoft Language Portal as handy reference

rules.description: Run spell and grammar checks before completing project
Pay attention to character conversion errors
Check for inconsistency, especially for katakana spelling
Refer to most updated version of approved RingCentral term list
rules.notes: Use trademark symbols only on first mention unless otherwise indicated

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: Follow localized equivalence in official websites if available for third party product names

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: Complete extensive research to determine if official approved trademarked translated version is available
rules.notes: Before using as in source

rules.description: Copyright notice ©2015 RingCentral, Inc. All rights reserved. should not be translated
The term 'copyright' should not be translated in copyright statements but should be translated as 著作権 elsewhere
rules.notes: When copyright appears outside of copyright statement

rules.description: If software has been translated, match relevant translation memory or string list provided
rules.notes: Bilingual format not needed for unknown UIs. Provide translation tentatively

rules.description: If software has not been translated, use bilingual format with English first, followed by target in brackets
